summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting featuring the ceremonial swearing-in of Officer Tyler Smith and the promotion of James Beckleheimer to Sergeant. The board approved a nuisance abatement resolution for properties on Clarewell Drive, approved out-of-town travel for fire department leadership, declared surplus vehicles, and opened street painting bids. Park board members Bill VanName and Dave Curry were reappointed retroactively.
decisions: Motion to adopt the evening's agenda – passed
Motion to approve payment of bills (June 9–22, 2021) – passed
Resolution OR-013-021 approved: nuisance abatement for parcels on Clarewell Drive – passed
Motion to approve out-of-town travel for Fire Chief to attend IAFC Conference July 27-30 at no cost – passed
Motion to approve out-of-town travel for Deputy Chief to attend FDIC conference in Indianapolis August 30 not to exceed $1,500 – passed
Motion to declare surplus and sell 2007 Chevrolet Tahoe (VIN ending 446) on GovDeals.com – passed
Motion to transfer 2008 Ford Escape from fire department to zoning department – passed
Motion to declare surplus and sell 2007 vehicle (VIN ending 7740, current zoning vehicle) on GovDeals.com – passed
Motion to reappoint Bill VanName and Dave Curry to Goshen Park Board retroactive to 5-10-2021 for three-year terms expiring 5-14-2024 – passed
Motion to adjourn at 8:25 PM – passed
action_items: Harry Hobert to notify property owner and lien holders regarding nuisance abatement on Clarewell Drive properties and meet with owner
Jeff (service department) to send updated road paving schedule/spreadsheet to trustees before July meeting
Staff to review street painting bids and make recommendations for July 6 meeting
Cheryl Allgaier to present contract negotiations at the next trustee meeting
Township officials urged to reach out to legislators regarding Senate Bill 52 (solar/wind facility siting)
Staff to research and set reserve prices on surplus vehicles for GovDeals.com
key_amounts: $1,500 – not-to-exceed amount for Deputy Chief travel to FDIC conference
$1.7 million – potential American Rescue Plan funding for the township if amendment is approved
notable_topics: Ceremonial swearing-in of Officer Tyler Smith
Promotion of James Beckleheimer to Sergeant
Nuisance abatement resolution for Clarewell Drive properties
Senate Bill 52 – township zoning authority over wind and solar facilities
American Rescue Plan (HB 111) amendment potentially including townships for direct federal stimulus funding
2021 street painting program bid opening (O'Banion Creek Lane, O'Banion Bluff, East Huntley Road, Gibson Road, Linton Road, Moeller Road)
Surplus and transfer of fire/zoning department vehicles
Contract negotiations in progress by HR/finance director
Goshen Park District kickball event planned for August 7
attendees: Julia Carney (Attorney, Clermont County Prosecutor's Office)
notes: Trustee Hausman participated remotely via Zoom from Florida, noted as one of the last times remote participation would be allowed. A 20-minute recess was taken for refreshments provided by the Citizens Police Academy Alumni Association. The board entered executive session at 8:15 PM per ORC 121.22(G)(1) to consider appointment of a public official and returned at 8:22 PM. Fire truck donation from Jericho Township was referenced. Deputy Chief plays bagpipes at line-of-duty funerals and ceremonies across Ohio.
title: BOT MTG 6.22.21
